text: The surgeon activates the near-infrared mode, revealing a signal in the anterior plane of dissection. The standard laparoscope emits near-infrared light, which penetrates tissue more effectively than white light, making the signal visible in the dissection area.
The surgical field is illuminated by near-infrared light as the abdominal team removes their light source from the abdomen, maintaining visibility of the dissection area.

The abdominal light source is reintroduced, enhancing the near-infrared light signal visible on the pinpoint display. As the abdominal and transanal teams approach the junction, the near-infrared signal intensifies, indicating proximity.
